filter_pushdown.note: lineitem is physically sorted by l_shipdate ascending; zone map skips ~7 trailing blocks (min > 10471) out of 600 total. 593 blocks scanned, ~59,316,666 rows pass.

logical_plan.group_by_note: l_returnflag has 3 codes (0=R,1=N,2=A), l_linestatus has 2 codes (0=F,1=O) -> 3x2=6 possible slots. Use flat 2D array indexed by [rf_code][ls_code]; zero hash overhead. Derive AVG from SUM/COUNT at output time.

data_structures.aggregation_detail: Flat 2D array of 6 AggSlot structs. Each slot holds: sum_qty, sum_base_price, sum_disc_price, sum_charge, sum_discount, count (all double/int64). Indexed by [rf_code][ls_code] directly — O(1) with no branching, no hashing. Thread-local copies merged sequentially after parallel phase.
data_structures.column_files: mmap_readonly — 7 column files memory-mapped for zero-copy sequential access

parallelism.merge_cost: trivial — 6 slots x 6 accumulators = 36 additions after parallel phase
parallelism.merge_note: Each thread maintains its own direct_array[3][2]; after all threads complete, a single sequential merge sums the 32 local arrays into one final array. No locking, no atomics needed during scan.

index_usage.layout: uint32_t num_blocks=600, then 600x{int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t block_size} (12 bytes each)
index_usage.benefit: minor (~1.2% blocks skipped) but zero-cost to apply; always use

physical_plan.scan_detail: One pass over all 7 column files simultaneously, block by block. For each block: (1) check zone map — skip if block_min > 10471; (2) inner loop over rows: test l_shipdate[i] <= 10471, if pass compute all aggregates and accumulate into local direct_array[rf][ls]. No intermediate materialization.

inner_loop_detail.group_key: group = rf_code * 2 + ls_code  (single multiply-add, avoids 2D indexing overhead)
inner_loop_detail.avg_derivation: Computed at output: avg_qty = sum_qty/count, avg_price = sum_price/count, avg_disc = sum_discount/count

physical_plan.memory_detail: All 7 column files are accessed sequentially block-by-block. Each column is a contiguous flat array — prefetch-friendly, no random access. With mmap and sequential access, OS read-ahead covers HDD latency. L3 cache (44MB) can hold ~1.5M rows of all 7 columns simultaneously (7 cols × 4-8 bytes × 1.5M ≈ 42MB), fitting a full morsel comfortably.

estimated_cost.bottleneck: HDD sequential read bandwidth across 7 columns x ~59.3M rows
estimated_cost.estimation_rationale: 32-thread parallel scan over 593 active blocks (~59.3M rows). Sequential HDD read at ~2-3 GB/s effective throughput for mmap. 2.26 GB / 2.5 GB/s ≈ 900ms single-threaded IO; with 32 parallel threads and OS read-ahead, effective ~80-90ms wall time. Merge phase: <1ms. Output sort of 6 rows: negligible. Target: match Umbra/DuckDB at 83ms.
